title: Tephroseris besseriana
taxonomy: Tephroseris besseriana is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Tephroseris, which is a group of plants that are closely related to the genus Cremanium. Tephroseris besseriana is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is also known as Besser's sericocarp or Besser's mountain cudweed. It is a perennial plant that thrives in areas with well-drained soil and full sun to partial shade.
uses: Tephroseris besseriana, also known as the Besser's mountain everlasting, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating wounds and infections. Additionally, Tephroseris besseriana is believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. intro: Tephroseris besseriana, also known as Besser's rock cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, including wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Tephroseris besseriana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
